title: Pandemic Contact Net: Spread-Out Superspreader-Safe Layout
strategies: shrunk-ring baseline: N attendees equally spaced on the radius-0.3 concentric ring (reproduces the checker baseline; min area dominated by 3-consecutive-vertex slivers)
full inscribed-circle ring (radius 0.5) plus multi-restart uniform random sampling in the square, keeping the layout with the largest minimum triangle area

bottleneck-driven local search: seed from the full inscribed ring, repeatedly locate the smallest-area triple and push one of its vertices perpendicular to the opposite edge, accepting only moves that raise the global minimum triangle area, over several seeded restarts
structured corner-loaded lattices / perturbed grids that fill the square and avoid thin triangles across the whole hall
